resources.string: current bands: UNKNOWN — refusing to swap
You\'re currently on %1$s bands. To swap to %2$s, the app needs the %2$s firmware blob. Get it once — after that, every swap is automatic.

Your phone is reporting bands that match neither US nor stock. The app refuses to touch a phone in this state — you\'d brick the modem. Make sure this is a Qin F21 (pre-v3) and that nothing else has modified the bands.
Loaded into app storage. You can delete the file you picked from your phone.

This will:

1. Back up your current %1$s bands (preserves your %1$s IMEI).
2. Flash %2$s bands to your modem.
3. Reboot.

The whole thing takes 1–3 minutes. DO NOT power off, unplug, or kill the app while it's running. A half-flash will brick your modem.
Done. Reboot to use the new bands.
The new bands won\'t take effect until reboot. The modem caches the old config until then.
Root not available. This app needs Magisk root.
Refusing to operate: current bands match neither US nor stock. See Info for details.
That file doesn\'t look like a valid F21 bands blob.
Swap failed: %1$s\n\nYour backup is still in app storage. See Info for recovery instructions.

EXCLUSIVELY for rooted DuoQin F21 prior to v3.

v3 has a RED charging port and serial number prefix F21PMQC25 — running this app on v3 will SOFT-BRICK THE MODEM.

If you're not sure, don't use this app.

Your previous bands were backed up before flashing. The backup blob lives at:

/data/data/com.flipphoneguy.f21bands/files/bands_<region>.tar.xz

Boot into recovery (or use any rooted shell), pull the blob, decompress with `tar -xJf`, and `dd` each .bin file to its partition (md1img_a, nvcfg, nvdata, nvram). The app will retry the same steps on next launch — open it again first before doing anything manual.
Modifying an IMEI is illegal in some jurisdictions. You are responsible for checking your local laws.
